_meta.purpose: Clinical reference data for threshold calibration. Every normalization constant and concern threshold must trace back to an entry here.

cadence.source: Dusing & Thorpe 2007; Sutherland 1988 — Pediatric gait development norms
cadence.note: Cadence counts left+right foot strikes per minute. Values outside physiological bounds indicate measurement error, not pathology.

stepTimingSymmetry.source: Robinson et al. 2006; Patterson et al. 2010 — gait symmetry indices
stepTimingSymmetry.note: Symmetry ratio = min(left, right) / max(left, right) of step durations.

frontalAsymmetry.source: Wren et al. 2005 — frontal-plane gait analysis; Baker 2006 — pelvic obliquity norms

hipHeightDifference.description: Absolute Y-difference between left and right hip in MediaPipe normalized coords
hipHeightDifference.note: In MediaPipe coords (0-1 screen), typical pelvic obliquity during normal gait causes ~0.005-0.010 Y-difference. Values >0.025 suggest clinically relevant pelvic drop.
hipHeightDifference.divisorJustification: Set so that 2 SD above pathological mean (0.025 + 2*0.005 = 0.035) maps to score = 1.0

shoulderTilt.description: Absolute Y-difference between left and right shoulder in MediaPipe normalized coords
shoulderTilt.note: Shoulders show more natural variation than hips. Clinically significant trunk lean involves >0.04 in normalized coords.
shoulderTilt.divisorJustification: Set so that moderate pathological shoulder tilt (0.06) maps to score = 1.0

frontalAsymmetry.note: Composite of hip height asymmetry (pelvic drop proxy) and shoulder tilt (trunk lean proxy). Both are frontal-plane valid.

strideRegularity.note: CV of step intervals. Healthy adults ~2-4%, neurological populations often >5%. Children naturally show higher variability.

lateralTrunkSway.source: Menz et al. 2003 — trunk sway during gait; Goutier et al. 2010

normativeSD.note: SD of lateral shoulder-hip offset in normalized MediaPipe coords during normal gait is ~0.004-0.008

lateralTrunkSway.divisorJustification: Set so that clinically obvious lateral instability (~0.025 SD) maps to score = 1.0. Previous value (0.04) was too generous — children with moderate sway were scoring too low.
lateralTrunkSway.note: Measures mediolateral trunk stability. In frontal view, this is one of the most reliable metrics.

normativeResidualsSD.note: Residual SD from linear regression of hip-center X over time in MediaPipe normalized coords. Healthy walkers typically <0.008

pathDeviation.divisorJustification: Set so that 3.5% of screen width deviation from a straight path maps to 1.0. Previous value (0.05) was too generous.
pathDeviation.note: Tracks how straight the walking path is. Ideal for frontal/away video.

kneeROM.source: Perry & Burnfield 2010 — Gait Analysis: Normal and Pathological Function
kneeROM.note: Used by the backend XGBoost pipeline, not the client-side frontal analysis.

minimumEvidenceRequirements.note: Minimum evidence required before reporting a metric with meaningful confidence. Below these thresholds, the metric should be marked as limited.
